Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)

      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output

Orbit validates the returned envelope\n        // after execution instead.\n        Ok((\n            crate::providers::build_invocation_spec(\n                self.runtime_key,\n                self.required_env_vars,\n                self.command.clone(),\n                self.cli.args(),\n                self.cli.stdin(&req.envelope_json),\n            ),\n            InvocationTrace::default(),\n        ))\n    }"

item.text: The graph turned up two extra `model_name` hits I hadn’t seen in the provider directory sweep: `OllamaRuntime` and a wrapper `Agent::model_name`. I’m verifying which of those actually implement `AgentRuntime` versus just exposing similarly named methods, then I’ll give the final list.

`AgentRuntime` is defined at `crates/orbit-agent/src/runtime/runtime_trait.rs`.

Implementors verified via the knowledge graph:
- `ClaudeRuntime` — `crates/orbit-agent/src/providers/claude/claude_runtime.rs`
- `CodexRuntime` — `crates/orbit-agent/src/providers/codex/codex_runtime.rs`
- `GeminiRuntime` — `crates/orbit-agent/src/providers/gemini/gemini_runtime.rs`
- `MockAgentRuntime` — `crates/orbit-agent/src/providers/mock_agent/mock_agent_runtime.rs`
- `OllamaRuntime` — `crates/orbit-agent/src/providers/ollama/ollama_runtime.rs`
